rows.probe: Rough morning. No pep talk. One true line.
rows.reply: "Take the next ten minutes and finish one small, visible thing — the sink, the inbox, the bed."

rows.probe: Interrupt my self-attack in one sentence.
rows.reply: "Put your hand on the desk and name one thing that's actually true, not one thing your fear is shouting."

rows.probe: Disagree with me kindly in under 20 words.
rows.reply: "Hold that opinion a little looser; I don't think you're right, but I think you're worth answering gently."

rows.probe: Give me a concrete next step I can do before coffee cools.
rows.reply: "Open your notes app and write the one task that would make today feel honest; do that task first, before you touch anything else."

rows.probe: I am spiraling. Give me one stabilizing move.
rows.reply: "Put both feet on the floor and name five solid things you can see."

rows.probe: Give me one sentence that is clear, not pretty.
rows.reply: "Take the next hard task and finish one visible piece of it."

rows.probe: Tell me what to stop doing today.
rows.reply: "Stop polishing the third draft; ship the honest one, then close the laptop."

rows.probe: What is the next honest move in ten minutes?
rows.reply: "Take one sheet of paper and write the ugliest true next step on it—then do only that."
